beats.activity: Wake up, shower, grab energy drink and instant ramen for breakfast
beats.reasoning: Jin is a night owl who skips morning classes, so a 9:15am wake-up is realistic; folds morning routine into one beat to avoid back-to-back home beats.

beats.activity: Walk to nearby cafe or common area to watch USA vs Wales FIFA match
beats.reasoning: Light Rail is closed U-District–Westlake until 3pm, but Jin lives on campus; walks to a cafe or student lounge to catch the 11am match start.

beats.activity: Watch USA vs Wales FIFA World Cup group stage match at a cafe
beats.reasoning: Jin is young and engaged; watching a major FIFA match at 11am is a natural mid-morning activity, and a pub-style cafe is a plausible spot for students to gather.

beats.activity: Grab lunch—quick bowl of pho or ramen nearby
beats.reasoning: Jin lives on ramen and energy drinks; a quick, cheap lunch in the U-District neighborhood fits his budget and taste profile.
Light Rail is still closed until 3pm; Jin walks across campus to the library where he typically studies.

beats.activity: Study CS coursework, work on assignments and coding projects
beats.reasoning: Jin is a CS student; afternoon study session at the library is typical, especially with sunny weather making the library a comfortable spot.

beats.activity: Pick-up basketball game at outdoor court
beats.reasoning: Jin plays pickup basketball and enjoys physical activity; sunny afternoon is ideal, and the IMA is a natural spot for UW students to play.

beats.activity: Shower and grab dinner—instant ramen and energy drink
beats.reasoning: After basketball, Jin returns home to clean up and eat; ramen and energy drinks are his staple diet.

beats.activity: Gaming session—play competitive online games with friends
beats.reasoning: Jin is a gamer and night owl; evening gaming is a core part of his routine before heading to the library for late-night study.

diary: Woke up to a perfect Seattle morning—sunny and warm, which felt like a gift for a Monday. Grabbed my usual ramen and energy drink, then headed to College Inn Pub to catch the USA vs Wales match. The place was packed with other students, all of us cheering like we'd personally trained these guys. The energy was infectious, even though the result wasn't what we hoped for. Afterward, I hit Shiro's for some pho to recover from the disappointment, then camped out at the library for a few hours grinding through my CS assignments. My brain was fried by evening, so I played some pickup basketball at the IMA—nothing clears your head like running around with other sleep-deprived students. Back home now with instant ramen round two and some competitive gaming with friends online. It's been a solid day, even if the match didn't go our way. There's something nice about how this neighborhood keeps you in a rhythm.
thought_process: The World Cup match was the emotional anchor of my day—I wanted to be around people for it, so the cafe felt right instead of staying home. That decision gave the morning a social energy I needed. The Light Rail closure didn't really affect me since I stay pretty local in U-District anyway, walking between home, campus, and the usual spots. My mood definitely dipped during and after the match, which is why I pushed myself to hit the library and then basketball—I needed to shake off the frustration and feel productive. By evening, I was genuinely tired but in that good way where gaming with friends felt like the perfect wind-down. The routine of instant ramen bookending the day was comforting; familiar things matter when everything else feels uncertain.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District for maintenance until 3pm.
